whatChanged: Registered shipped systems in Foundation Systems/source truth, starting with the missing GLS system record.
whatItDoes: Adds SYS-SALES-GLS-001 as GLS System / Get Listings Sold under Sales, keeps Agent Onboarding Feedback visible as live, separates ClickUp source truth from KPI Shopping List supporting evidence, and adds a shipped-system registration check so protected closed systems fail if missing from groupedSystems.
whyItMatters: Closed operating systems should be discoverable from Foundation Systems without Steve remembering which hub page or chat proved them.
whereItLives: Foundation > Systems > Sales > GLS System / Get Listings Sold
Foundation > Systems > Agent Onboarding > Agent Onboarding Feedback System
proofStatus: Proof checks SYS-SALES-GLS-001 in groupedSystems, Sales service-area placement, live implementation state, /sales GLS routes, ClickUp Deal Data Entry source truth, KPI Shopping List supporting evidence, SALES-GLS-SCOREBOARD-V1 closeout proof, Agent Onboarding Feedback still visible/live, and shipped-system registration verifier coverage.
knownLimits: This is a registration and verifier sweep only; it does not build new GLS features.
This does not build new onboarding features.
This does not start Strategy, Scoper, Agent, corpus, or broad Foundation cleanup.
Closeout owns only SYSTEM-REGISTRATION-SWEEP-001.

whatChanged: Paused SOURCE-021 honestly after closing the read/coaching semantics and identifying the exact missing proof for writer ownership.
whatItDoes: Locks the plain-English FUB/KPI lead semantics: leaddate means opportunity-entry timing, leadclaimeddate means claim/recycle timing, Active Client is downstream opportunity-path context, and coaches must not say an agent created a lead unless a brand-new human is proven. The card stays scoped until the production writer/replication path into live Supabase is proven.
whyItMatters: Sales Hub and future coaches cannot overstate lead creation or rebuild a live KPI/FUB writer path from partial evidence. The system now knows what it may read and explain, and what proof is still missing before writes are allowed.
proofStatus: Writer proof check passes with the scoped conclusion: fub.up_InsertPerson explains LeadDate and LeadClaimedDate, checked-in direct Supabase sync does not write those rich date fields, live Supabase has current leaddate/leadclaimeddate values, and exact production writer/replication path remains missing.
reviewNext: Plan SECURITY-002 only: real auth/tier/redaction before any more hub/query/assistant expansion. Do not code SECURITY-002 until Steve approves the 9.8 plan.
knownLimits: SOURCE-021 is not done.
The exact production writer/replication path into live Supabase remains unproven.
This does not rebuild or write the KPI/FUB person pipeline.
This does not implement SECURITY-002, Strategy Hub, Sales expansion, Agent Feedback expansion, Scoper, Agent Factory, broad corpus expansion, or UI polish.

whatChanged: Implemented the SECURITY-002 auth/tier/redaction foundation from the approved plan.
whatItDoes: Adds a central access layer with server-derived actor context, DB-backed tier resolution, assertTier/assertRole helpers, a route posture registry for every approved read surface, stable redacted response helpers, subject_people/sensitivity/min_tier filtering, and owner-preserving shared-comms summary boundaries. Routes that cannot yet be safely filtered remain Tier 1-only.
whyItMatters: Broad hub, query, and assistant expansion cannot proceed while sensitive shared communications can leak through ad hoc route checks or client-provided tier values. This makes the default fail closed and proves external/non-Steve users cannot read Steve/person-sensitive Tier 1 data.
proofStatus: Focused proof covers Steve/Tier 1, Ryan/Tier 2, Georgia/Tier 3, John external/missing-tier denial, local dev Tier 1, admin token as system source, tier predicate semantics, subject-person redaction, missing classification fail-closed behavior, stable redacted shapes, shared-comms raw-content boundary, full route posture registry coverage, and server-side evidence tier derivation. foundation:verify covers the same layer durably.
reviewNext: Stop for Steve review. Do not start Strategy Hub, Sales expansion, Agent Feedback expansion, Scoper, Agent Factory, corpus expansion, broad query surfaces, or UI polish from this closeout.
knownLimits: Non-Tier-1 shared-comms and intelligence access remains closed unless a later approved card proves filtered summaries on real data.
This does not build a user admin panel; FOUNDATION-USERS-001 remains the smaller owner-only follow-up.
This does not migrate raw meeting-note vault storage or broaden raw-source access.
This does not correct every legacy internal maxTier query path; human-facing evidence access now derives tier server-side and fail-closes through the route posture.

whatChanged: Implemented the Foundation readiness exit test from the approved FOUNDATION-DONE-TEST-001 plan.
whatItDoes: Adds a central readiness registry and process command that evaluate source-verifiable answers, tier/redaction safety, P0 gate coverage, runtime/process health, extraction retry/ledger/backfill health, raw meeting Drive ACL/vault status, and stable pass/fail output. The gate can honestly report not_ready while naming blocker cards.
whyItMatters: Foundation now has a concrete exit test instead of a chat-based feeling of done. Strategy should resume only after this gate says ready, or after Steve explicitly accepts the named blocker state.
proofStatus: The readiness command is expected to report not_ready until the named blocker cards close; report-only proof verifies the detector and output shape without pretending Foundation is ready.
reviewNext: Stop for Steve review. Next pull one named blocker card from the readiness output, likely SYSTEM-010-GHOST-CLOSEOUT-001, EXTRACT-RUN-HARDENING-001, SOURCE-LIFECYCLE-COMPLETION-001, SYNTHESIS-VERIFY-001, or MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001.
knownLimits: This closes the exit-test implementation, not Foundation readiness.
The first valid output is expected to be not_ready while blocker cards remain open.

This does not reopen SECURITY-002, mutate Drive ACLs, broaden public/external access, start Strategy, expand Sales or Agent Feedback, build Scoper/Agent Factory, expand corpus lanes, or add UI polish.

whatChanged: Implemented the approved SYSTEM-010-GHOST-CLOSEOUT-001 runtime/process-control closeout slice.
whatItDoes: Adds a central runtime process-control layer, active-process view, liveness/dead-man rollup, owned PID stop decision path, confirmation-gated decommission control, decommissioned job runtime mode, auto-restart-on-push status reporting, cost/process risk rollup, Runtime Health controls, focused proof script, verifier hooks, and readiness-gate integration.
whyItMatters: Foundation can no longer pass runtime/process readiness while stale or unowned jobs can run silently. Running work, stop eligibility, restart status, and spend/process risk are visible and fail closed.
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval integrity, central runtime process-control policy, active-process and route shape, decommission fail-closed behavior, unowned PID denial, owned fixture process termination, package wiring, build-log closeout, and foundation verifier/readiness hooks.
reviewNext: Foundation readiness should still report not_ready while SOURCE-LIFECYCLE-COMPLETION-001, SYNTHESIS-VERIFY-001, EXTRACT-RUN-HARDENING-001, MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001, and DRIVE-ACCESS-REQUEST-001 remain open.
knownLimits: This does not mark SOURCE-LIFECYCLE-COMPLETION-001, SYNTHESIS-VERIFY-001, EXTRACT-RUN-HARDENING-001, MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001, or DRIVE-ACCESS-REQUEST-001 done.
Auto-restart-on-push is reported honestly as manual_restart_required unless a later card proves a push hook or LaunchAgent WatchPaths restart path.
Stop controls fail closed for active runs without owned PID metadata instead of guessing at arbitrary system processes.

This does not start Strategy, Sales expansion, Agent Feedback expansion, Scoper, Agent Factory, broad corpus/video mining, YouTube scout expansion, researcher/self-improvement agent work, meeting Drive ACL work, or UI polish beyond necessary Runtime Health safety controls.

whatChanged: Implemented the approved SOURCE-LIFECYCLE-COMPLETION-001 source completion and revalidation closeout slice.
whatItDoes: Adds a central source lifecycle completion registry and process proof that classify all 35 source contracts into terminal states, prove all 12 governed extraction targets remain represented with unchanged caps, and keep future/gap sources accepted-blocked with owners, reasons, next actions, and blocker cards.
whyItMatters: Foundation readiness should not treat vague Pending Revalidation or Gap source states as healthy. This makes current load-bearing sources explicit while preventing future connector ideas from masquerading as Strategy-readiness blockers.
proofStatus: Focused proof requires 35/35 source contracts to have terminal states, 22 load-bearing sources to be complete/read-only/current-reality, 13 future/gap sources to be accepted-blocked with owner/reason/next action/blocker card, all 12 extraction targets to remain represented, target baselines/caps unchanged, and metadata-only output with zero raw/private content leaks.
reviewNext: Foundation readiness should still report not_ready while SYNTHESIS-VERIFY-001, EXTRACT-RUN-HARDENING-001, MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001, and DRIVE-ACCESS-REQUEST-001 remain open. Next blocker should be chosen from that readiness output.
knownLimits: This proves source lifecycle completion for the current role of each source; it does not perform claim-level synthesis verification.
Accepted-blocked future/gap sources are not built or marked complete. Their blocker cards remain future work.

Drive rich media/ACL gaps, YouTube scout/rich video understanding, Mycro, Skool, Loom, Google Ads, publishing, reviews, training, content, Real Broker, and team-wide email connector builds remain out of scope.

This does not implement extraction retry/backoff, meeting Drive ACL/vault changes, Strategy, Sales expansion, Agent Feedback expansion, Scoper, Agent Factory, broad corpus/video mining, researcher/self-improvement agent work, sprint view, or UI polish.

whatChanged: Implemented the approved SYNTHESIS-VERIFY-001 synthesized-claim verification closeout slice.
whatItDoes: Adds a central synthesis claim verifier, stamps verification metadata onto governed synthesized items/shared-comms synthesized items/action routes, blocks unsupported/stale/contradicted/missing-tier and single-evidence Strategy-grade claims, requires verified synthesized items before Action Router proposal, refuses unverified decision-grade route approval/apply, filters Strategy Hub v2 to verified Strategy routes, and keeps shared-comms legacy synthesis advisory/blocked when it cannot be verified.
whyItMatters: Foundation can no longer treat generated synthesis as decision-grade because the wording sounds plausible. Strategy, future scouts, researchers, and advisor-style output must stand on source IDs, facts, retrieval chunks, and verified provenance.
whereItLives: server.js Strategy Hub v2 route filtering
proofStatus: Focused proof checks synthetic unsupported/stale/contradicted/missing-tier/single-evidence failures, live governed synthesis verification, shared-comms advisory downgrade, decision-grade route verification, metadata-only output, and readiness-gate integration.
reviewNext: Foundation readiness should still report not_ready while EXTRACT-RUN-HARDENING-001, MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001, and DRIVE-ACCESS-REQUEST-001 remain open. Next blocker should be chosen from that readiness output.
knownLimits: This verifies or downgrades existing synthesis; it does not build new Strategy, scout, researcher, Scoper, or advisor output.
Legacy shared-comms LLM synthesis can remain advisory/blocked when candidate/source refs are not enough for decision-grade proof.
This does not implement extraction retry/backoff, meeting Drive ACL/vault changes, public access, broad corpus/video mining, sprint view, or UI polish.

whatChanged: Implemented the approved EXTRACT-RUN-HARDENING-001 retry, ledger, stale-lease, partial-failure, and bounded-backfill closeout slice.
whatItDoes: Adds a central extraction hardening policy, queryable retry state on source_crawl_items, item-attempt ledger rows tied to crawlRunId, runner crawlRunId propagation, idempotent target-run finish handling, stale item-lease reaping, retry/backoff classification, target next-safe-command visibility, Runtime Health retry/exhausted/blocked/stale counts, focused proof script, verifier coverage, and readiness-gate integration.
whyItMatters: Foundation can no longer treat extraction as healthy while failed items, stale item leases, duplicate attempts, partial runs, or unbounded backfill work can hide underneath a target-level status.
whereItLives: lib/foundation-db.js source_crawl_items retry columns and source_crawl_item_attempts
public/foundation.js Runtime Health extraction retry state
proofStatus: Focused proof checks synthetic retry/backoff and duplicate-attempt behavior, live target representation, active backfill/corpus/recovery bounds, stale target and item leases, classified failed items, partial/failed next-safe commands, metadata-only output, and readiness-gate clearance.
reviewNext: Foundation readiness should still report not_ready while MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001 and DRIVE-ACCESS-REQUEST-001 remain open. Next blocker should be meeting raw Drive ACL/vault safety or its delegated Drive access preflight.
knownLimits: This hardens existing extraction lanes only; it does not build new connectors or widen corpus scope.
Unsafe item retry paths stay blocked with a next-safe action instead of rerunning broad windows.

This does not mutate meeting Drive ACLs, build Strategy, Sales expansion, Agent Feedback expansion, Scoper, Agent Factory, broad corpus/video mining, researcher/self-improvement agents, public access, sprint view, or UI polish.

whatChanged: Implemented the approved DRIVE-ACCESS-REQUEST-001 delegated Drive dry-run/preflight closeout slice.
whatItDoes: Adds a central Drive access preflight layer that proves the actor shape, reads metadata-only file and permission inventory, classifies missing access, owner ambiguity, request-access-needed, unsafe shares, and repairability, writes dry-run ledger rows, and blocks any email or Google Drive permission mutation in this slice.
whyItMatters: Foundation can distinguish "we need access or approval" from "raw meeting files are safe" before any meeting ACL repair work. That prevents the system from pretending raw Google Drive access is protected just because AIOS-side redaction exists.
proofStatus: Focused proof checks synthetic Drive preflight fixtures, live actor proof, metadata-only meeting file permission preflight, dry-run ledger writes, no raw/private proof output, no request-access emails, no Drive permission mutations, and readiness-gate clearance for DRIVE-ACCESS-REQUEST-001.
reviewNext: MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001 remains the raw meeting Drive ACL/vault blocker unless Phase A proves every file safe or a separately approved Phase B mutates, rechecks, and records rollback proof.
knownLimits: This closes preflight only. It does not prove raw meeting files are already safe.
This does not send request-access emails or create, delete, update, or transfer Google Drive permissions.
Real Drive permission mutation remains blocked pending a separate MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001 Phase B approval tied to a Phase A dry-run hash.

This does not build Strategy, Sales expansion, Agent Feedback expansion, Scoper, Agent Factory, broad corpus/video mining, researcher/self-improvement agents, extraction work, filtered comms access, public access, sprint view, or UI polish.

whatChanged: Implemented the approved FOUNDATION-SPRINT-SYSTEM-001 Current Sprint execution-control overlay.
whatItDoes: Adds a central sprint registry, additive sprint overlay tables, live-backlog-backed API payload, compact Recent Work panel, Sprint Ready existing-work/doctrine checks, returned-reason enforcement, not-next boundaries, and focused process proof.
whyItMatters: Foundation now has a visible active-sprint control layer so the next card, blocker, proof commands, and boundaries do not live only in chat while done cards still flow into Recent Work below.
whereItLives: public/foundation.js Current Sprint panel at top of Recent Work
proofStatus: Focused proof checks the stage registry, no-second-backlog data model, Sprint Ready doctrine fields, returned reason requirement, Recent Work rendering hook, follow-up boundaries, live backlog reconciliation, metadata-only proof, and served API payload.
reviewNext: Return to MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001 through the new Sprint Ready existing-work/doctrine check. Phase B remains paused: do not mutate Drive permissions or send request-access emails without separate approval.
knownLimits: This is a Current Sprint overlay only. It does not replace the live backlog.
Done-velocity graphing stays scoped under FOUNDATION-DONE-VELOCITY-001 unless reliable data is cheap and honest.
Broader Foundation UI polish stays scoped under FOUNDATION-SURFACE-UPDATES-001.

This does not build MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001 Phase B, mutate Google Drive permissions, send request-access emails, or start Strategy, Sales expansion, Agent Feedback expansion, Scoper, Agent Factory, broad corpus/video mining, researcher/self-improvement agents, filtered comms access, public access, or sprint-view work.

whatChanged: Implemented the FOUNDATION-SPRINT-CADENCE-001 Current Sprint command view.
whatItDoes: Adds an executive sprint summary, sprint goal, current status, next card, current blocker, exit criteria, readable stage rows, definition of done, proof commands, returned reason, and next action at the top of Recent Work.
whyItMatters: Steve can scan the sprint command state without inferring the plan from skinny cards, chat prompts, or individual backlog rows.
whereItLives: lib/foundation-current-sprint.js cadence payload and exit criteria
proofStatus: Focused proof checks the central cadence payload, stage order, exit criteria, readable command-board layout, approval artifact, live backlog reconciliation, no raw/private proof output, served API cadence summary, and no-Drive/no-email boundaries.
reviewNext: Return to MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001 under Sprint Ready rules. Phase B remains paused: no Drive permission mutation or request-access email is approved.
knownLimits: This is sprint command visibility only, not broad Foundation UI polish.
Done-velocity graphing remains scoped under FOUNDATION-DONE-VELOCITY-001.
MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001 moves into Scoping as the next visible work while remaining the active Foundation blocker until a separate approved path resolves it.

No Drive permission mutation, request-access email, Strategy, Sales expansion, Agent Feedback expansion, Scoper, Agent Factory, broad corpus/video mining, researcher/self-improvement agent, public access, or broad sprint analytics shipped in this card.

whatChanged: Closed the GLS Scoreboard V1 review, sped up the Sales Hub default load path, clarified source freshness/save behavior, and captured the unbuilt GLS ideas into backlog truth.
whatItDoes: Keeps GLS as one Sales Hub system with a case-first dashboard, manager queue, leader scoreboard, case history, cached AIOS view, last-refresh readout, manual ClickUp refresh, and follow-up cards for split/merge, re-stale reopen, manager usability, accountability, filters, and history controls.
whyItMatters: Sales leadership can use GLS without opening ClickUp or waiting on every live ClickUp pull, while future ideas from the review are no longer trapped in chat.
proofStatus: Sales Hub process check verifies the GLS dashboard/manager, John and Carson access expectations, grouped Nick case proof, sold/closed visibility, cached AIOS view, manual refresh path, and no Open ClickUp View CTA. Backlog hygiene is green; seed drift has no new stable drift for the captured GLS cards. foundation:verify passes 232/232 with dashboard and worker serving this closeout commit.
reviewNext: Stop for Steve review. Next likely GLS card is SALES-GLS-GROUPING-OVERRIDES-001 if grouped-case trust blocks usage, or SALES-GLS-LEADER-ACCOUNTABILITY-001 if leadership wants the weekly accountability rhythm next.
knownLimits: Manual split/merge overrides are not built yet.
Re-stale after adjustment reopen behavior is not built yet.
Date filters, history cleanup controls, and the larger GROW Sales Dashboard remain future cards.
This does not build a CRM, agent coach, GBS, Strategy, Scoper, or source expansion.

whatChanged: Implemented the MEETING-VAULT-AUTO-ENFORCEMENT-001 report-only forward-flow control layer and close rule for MEETING-VAULT-ACL-001.
whatItDoes: Proves original Gemini notes are source truth, duplicate Google Docs are blocked, ACL decisions exclude legacy Crewbert copies, new notes are classified/preflighted, Crewbert and high-risk access actions are queued, and historical messy files are bounded in a legacy exception queue instead of driving endless manual permission batches.
whyItMatters: Foundation readiness needs a system that prevents new raw meeting-note exposure automatically. Historical Drive cleanup remains visible work, but it no longer blocks the operating system once the forward-flow guard is proven.
whereItLives: lib/foundation-readiness-gates.js meeting raw Drive ACL/vault close rule
public/foundation.js Runtime Health > Meeting Vault Auto-Enforcement
proofStatus: Focused proof checks synthetic forward-flow decisions, no-duplicate Google Docs rule, metadata-only output, database audit/exception rows, hub/dashboard visibility, readiness integration, and fail-closed apply/live mode.
reviewNext: Foundation readiness may now pass if all gates stay green. Historical Meeting Vault exceptions remain queued for a later explicitly approved cleanup or owner-authority repair sprint.
knownLimits: This is report-only automatic enforcement. It does not mutate Google Drive permissions.

No historical cleanup batch, Gmail guest removal, request-access email, file delete, move, ownership transfer, Strategy, Sales expansion, Agent Feedback expansion, Scoper, Agent Factory, broad corpus/video mining, researcher/self-improvement agent, public access, or broad UI polish shipped in this card.
Legacy exceptions are bounded and visible, not declared safe.

whatChanged: Closed the Foundation operator-surface follow-up by adding a plain-English Overview path, clickable Recent Work where-it-lives breadcrumbs, and an honest done-date signal in Backlog.
whatItDoes: Overview now tells Steve to move through Overview -> Systems -> Backlog -> Recent Work. Recent Work links safe app/doc/backlog destinations from closeout where-it-lives metadata while leaving repo/proof paths visible as labels. Backlog done rows show a moved-to-done or done/last-updated signal instead of hiding date context.
whyItMatters: Foundation should work as the CEO dashboard for system-building. Steve can see what exists, what is queued, what shipped, where it lives, and what proof backs it without reading chat history or translating raw file paths.
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval integrity, Plan Critic, live Current Sprint ownership, Overview operator-path markers, Recent Work location-link renderer, Backlog done-date signal, verifier transition from scoped to done, closeout registry wiring, and all-done sprint posture.
reviewNext: Roll the next approved Foundation sprint from live backlog truth and continue unattended safe work under the park-and-continue rule.
knownLimits: This is a narrow operator-surface clarity slice, not a broad dashboard redesign.
Done-velocity graphing remains future work unless the next sprint explicitly pulls it forward.
This does not build source, extractor, agent, value, external write, Drive permission, credential, paid/provider, or browser-auth work.
